channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Paizo Inc. Lays Off 12 Employees As Result of Diamond Comics Bankruptcy
item.content:encoded:
The studio behind Pathfinder has announced some layoffs. Paizo Inc. has stated that, as a result of financial strain from the bankruptcy of Diamond Comics, the studio has fired twelve of its employees.

The Recent Paizo Layoffs

Paizo Inc transparently addressed the reason for its recent layoffs in an official blog post. The post states the layoffs were a result of mitigating financial losses due to the bankruptcy of Diamond Comics. This is relevant because Diamond handled the shipping of products for several major outlets like Amazon and Barnes & Noble as well as hundreds of smaller studios like Paizo.

The financial losses on Paizo's part is because the agreement the company made with Diamond was based on consignment. Paizo pays Diamond to ship Paizo products to Diamond's warehouses. Paizo gets paid when said products are sold and shipped from Diamond's warehouses. 

This is relevant because, as a means of liquidating assets, the bank JP Morgan Chase has claimed a lien on all product in Diamond's inventory, including $10 million worth of Paizo product, meaning all of that product cannot be accessed by the publisher. Paizo, and other smaller publishers like Green Ronin, have been fighting this decision in court, attempting to reclaim ownership of their products.

Finally, there's the matter of Paizo having an exclusive distribution contract with Diamond. This means that, due to bankruptcy process, Diamond cannot sell any of Paizo's books, and because of the contract Paizo cannot move to another distributor. Complicating matters further is, while the contract was was terminated by a judge earlier this year, Diamond appealed the decision, delaying the resolution of the decision.

In short, due to a combination of court fees, a cratering of physical product sales, and the morass of legal procedure has put Paizo in a financial vice, and a sad reality of offsetting these losses involves letting people go. As a result, Paizo Inc and the United Paizo Workers union have put together a procedure to layoff twelve employees. Said employees will receive severance pay as well as 18-month recall rights.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/paizo-inc-lays-off-12-employees-as-result-of-diamond-comics-bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
